mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of the Elastic Stack on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic products in a Kubernetes environment, providing features like TLS management and persistent storage. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing Kubernetes resources and the need for familiarity with the Elastic Stack.

steps.title: Add the ECK Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This is the official Helm repo for ECK.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This command installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace elastic-system

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the ECK operator.
> Installs the ECK operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Verify the ECK operator installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the ECK operator is running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n elastic-system

```

> Lists all pods in the elastic-system namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Deploy an Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an Elasticsearch cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

```

> Creates an Elasticsearch cluster with 3 nodes.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Elasticsearch cluster to ensure it is running.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> Lists all Elasticsearch resources in the elastic-system namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator --namespace elastic-system

kubectl delete namespace elastic-system

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up existing resources, upgrading the operator, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > backup-elasticsearch.yaml

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

kubectl get pods -n elastic-system

```

> Backs up the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the ECK operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o yaml > backup-elasticsearch.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://download.elastic.co/downloads/eck/2.14.0/crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch pods may be in a crash loop or not starting due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n elastic-system' and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kibana is not accessible'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kibana service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Kibana service type is set to 'LoadBalancer' or 'NodePort'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm install failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm version may be incompatible or the repository may not be updated.

**Fix:**
Update Helm with 'helm repo update' and ensure Helm is at least version 3.x.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 9.x, improved TLS management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.13, ensure all Elasticsearch clusters are compatible with version 9.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster status as 'Green' when running 'kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: data
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher with sufficient resources.
